Name: PostgreSQL Multiple Vulnerabilities (Aug 2025) - Linux
description: PostgreSQL is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: PostgreSQL prior to version 13.22, 14.x prior to 14.19, 15.x prior to 15.14, 16.x prior to 16.10 and 17.x prior to 17.6.
insight: The following aws exist:
- CVE-2025-8713: Optimizer statistics can expose sampled data within a view, partition, or child table
- CVE-2025-8714: pg_dump lets superuser of origin server execute arbitrary code in psql client
- CVE-2025-8715: pg_dump newline in object name executes arbitrary code in psql client and in restore target server

Name: PostgreSQL End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux
description: The PostgreSQL version on the remote host has reached the End of Life (EOL) and should not be used anymore.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_result: The "PostgreSQL" version on the remote host has reached the end of life.
detection_method: Checks if an EOL version is present on the target host.
Details: PostgreSQL End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux
impact: An EOL version of PostgreSQL is not receiving any security updates from the vendor. Unxed security vulnerabilities might be leveraged by an attacker to compromise the security of this host.
solution: Update the PostgreSQL version on the remote host to a still supported version.

Name: PostgreSQL < 10.6, 11.x < 11.1 SQL Injection Vulnerability - Linux
description: PostgreSQL is prone to an SQL injection vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: PostgreSQL < 10.6, 11.x < 11.1 SQL Injection Vulnerability - Linux OID:1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.112429 Version used: 2024-07-19T05:05:32Z
insight: A SQL Injection aw has been discovered in PostgreSQL server in the way triggers that enable transition relations are dumped. The transition relation name is not correctly quoted and it may allow an attacker with CREATE privilege on some non-temporary schema or TRIGGER privilege on some table to create a malicious trigger that, when dumped and restored, would result in additional SQL statements being executed.

Name: PostgreSQL Default Credentials (PostgreSQL Protocol)
description: It was possible to login into the remote PostgreSQL as user postgres using weak credentials.
detection_result: It was possible to login as user postgres with password "postgres".
solution: Change the password as soon as possible.

Name: PostgreSQL < 9.6.24, 10.x < 10.19, 11.x < 11.14, 12.x < 12.9, 13.x < 13.5, 14.x < 14.1 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
description: PostgreSQL is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Aected Software/OS PostgreSQL prior to version 9.6.24, version 10.x through 10.18, 11.x through 11.13, 12.x through 12.8, 13.x through 13.4 and version 14.0.
insight: The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2021-23214: Server processes unencrypted bytes from man-in-the-middle
- CVE-2021-23222: libpq processes unencrypted bytes from man-in-the-middle

Name: PostgreSQL SQLi Vulnerability (Feb 2025) - Linux
description: PostgreSQL is prone to an SQL injection (SQLi) vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: PostgreSQL prior to version 13.19, 14.x prior to 14.16, 15.x prior to 15.11, 16.x prior to 16.7 and
insight: Improper neutralization of quoting syntax in PostgreSQL libpq functions PQescapeLiteral(),
PQescapeIdentier(), PQescapeString(), and PQescapeStringConn() allows a database input
provider to achieve SQL injection in certain usage patterns. Specically, SQL injection requires
the application to use the function result to construct input to psql, the PostgreSQL interac-
tive terminal. Similarly, improper neutralization of quoting syntax in PostgreSQL command
line utility programs allows a source of command line arguments to achieve SQL injection when
client_encoding is BIG5 and server_encoding is one of EUC_TW or MULE_INTERNAL.

Name: PostgreSQL < 9.5.23, 9.6.x < 9.6.19, 10.x < 10.14, 11.x < 11.9, 12.x < 12.4 Search Path Vulnerability - Linux
description: PostgreSQL is prone to an uncontrolled search path element vulnerability in CREATE EXTENSION.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: When a superuser runs certain CREATE EXTENSION statements, users may be able to execute arbitrary SQL functions under the identity of that superuser. The attacker must have permission to create objects in the new extension's schema or a schema of a prerequisite extension. Not all extensions are vulnerable.
insight: Aected Software/OS PostgreSQL versions prior to 9.5.23, 9.6.x prior to 9.6.19, 10.x prior to 10.14, 11.x prior to 11.9 and 12.x prior to 12.4.

Name: PostgreSQL < 9.4.26, 9.5.x < 9.5.21, 9.6.x < 9.6.17, 10.x < 10.12, 11.x < 11.7, 12.x < 12.2 Authorization Check Vulnerability - Linux
description: PostgreSQL is prone to an authorization check vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: The ALTER ... DEPENDS ON EXTENSION sub-commands do not perform authorization checks, which can allow an unprivileged user to drop any function, procedure, materialized view, index, or trigger under certain conditions. This attack is possible if an administrator has installed an extension and an unprivileged user can CREATE, or an extension owner either executes DROP EXTENSION predictably or can be convinced to execute DROP EXTENSION.
solution: Aected Software/OS PostgreSQL versions prior to 9.4.26, 9.5.x prior to 9.5.21, 9.6.x prior to 9.6.17, 10.x prior to 10.12, 11.x prior to 11.7 and 12.x prior to 12.2.

Name: PostgreSQL DoS Vulnerability (Feb 2025) - Linux
description: PostgreSQL is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability due to a buer over-read.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: A buer over-read in PostgreSQL GB18030 encoding validation allows a database input provider to achieve temporary denial of service on platforms where a 1-byte over-read can elicit process termination.
insight: A buer over-read in PostgreSQL GB18030 encoding validation allows a database input provider to achieve temporary denial of service on platforms where a 1-byte over-read can elicit process termination.

Name: PostgreSQL Multiple Vulnerabilities (Nov 2025) - Linux
description: PostgreSQL is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: - CVE-2025-12817: CREATE STATISTICS does not check for schema CREATE privilege
- CVE-2025-12818: libpq undersizes allocations, via integer wraparound
insight: The following aws exist:
- CVE-2025-12817: CREATE STATISTICS does not check for schema CREATE privilege
- CVE-2025-12818: libpq undersizes allocations, via integer wraparound

Name: PostgreSQL < 9.5.25, 9.6.x < 9.6.21, 10.x < 10.16, 11.x < 11.11, 12.x < 12.6, 13.x < 13.2 Information Disclosure Vulnerability - Linux
description: PostgreSQL is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: Successful exploitation would allow an attacker to read sensitive information.
insight: The following aws exist:
- CVE-2025-12817: CREATE STATISTICS does not check for schema CREATE privilege
- CVE-2025-12818: libpq undersizes allocations, via integer wraparound

Name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
description: The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.
detection_result: The following response / ICMP packet has been received:

Name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
description: The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: The following response / ICMP packet has been received:
detection_method: Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.
Details: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
impact: This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.
solution: - Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a rewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the rewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)
insight: The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.

Name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
description: The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between:

Name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
description: The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.
detection_result: It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between: Packet 1: 3098322066 Packet 2: 3098323154
detection_method: Special IP packets are forged and sent with a little delay in between to the target IP. The responses are searched for a timestamps. If found, the timestamps are reported.
impact: A side effect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.
solution: To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.

The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.
insight: The remote host implements TCP timestamps, as defined by RFC1323/RFC7323.

Name: PostgreSQL Detection (TCP)
description: TCP based detection of PostgreSQL.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: A PostgreSQL service has been identified on this port.
log_method: The script sends a connection request to the server (user:postgres, DB:postgres) and attempts to extract the version number from the reply.

Name: Services
description: This plugin performs service detection.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: An unknown service is running on this port.
It is usually reserved for Postgres
insight: This plugin attempts to guess which service is running on the remote port(s). For instance, it searches for a web server which could listen on another port than 80 or 443 and makes this information available for other check routines.

Name: Hostname Determination Reporting
description: The script reports information on how the hostname of the target was determined.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%

Name: PostgreSQL Detection Consolidation
description: Consolidation of PostgreSQL detections.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: select version(); query result: T versionDPostgreSQL 9.3.25 on x86_64-pc-linux-g

Name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting
description: This script consolidates the OS information detected by several VTs and tries to nd the best matching OS.
Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection.
If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the referenced community forum.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: Concluded from ICMP based OS fingerprint
Setting key "Host/runs_unixoide" based on this information
log_method: Details: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting

Name: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check
description: Checks if the remote host has IP forwarding enabled.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 70%
detection_result: It was possible to route a TCP packet through the target host and received an an
->swer which means IP forwarding is enabled.
log_method: Sends a crafted Local Link Layer (LLL) frame and checks the response.
Details: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check

Name: Traceroute
description: Collect information about the network route and network distance between the scanner host and the target host.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: Network route from scanner (172.30.0.2) to target (172.31.1.28):
Network distance between scanner and target: 2
insight: For internal networks, the distances are usually small, often less than 4 hosts between scanner and target. For public targets the distance is greater and might be 10 hosts or more.
log_method: A combination of the protocols ICMP and TCP is used to determine the route. This method is applicable for IPv4 only and it is also known as 'traceroute'.

Name: CPE Inventory
description: This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

Note: Some CPEs for specic products might show up twice or more in the output. Background: After a product got renamed or a specic vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older CPE.
